title: A Rooftop Clash Against Kaido and Big Mom
description: On pages 16–19, this part focuses on Unknown person #17, Unknown person #18, Unknown person #19, Unknown person #20, Zoro, Kid as The Worst Generation confronts Kaido and Big Mom on Onigashima.
scene: On the rooftop of Onigashima, the Worst Generation challenges Kaido and Big Mom directly, turning the raid into a clash against emperors.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: I'd go to sleep each night...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: wondering if I'd go to my daughter's bed the next morning

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ....only to find she hadn't woken up. That she'd never say "good morning" to me again.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Closing my eyes was terrifying, let me tell you.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I had wanted to do something special for her on the biggest day of her life...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...but hurting myself like this was the heavens telling me not to bother! That has to be it!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: Mr. Rulbei ...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Sorry for all the trouble I caused you folks.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll go tell your cook he can quit botherin' now.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: don't.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: Zoro?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What right do you have to talk, kid?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's my decision! You don't know a thing about this!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #18
dialogue.text: Oh, I know plenty.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Cooks and swordsmen may be totally different jobs...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...but we both make our living by the blade.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: When a man says he's gotta cut somethin'...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #18
dialogue.text: ...he's not going to back down.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: All of my knives are in top condition.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #19
dialogue.text: The fault can't lie with my tools.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What the heck do I have to do to filet this thing?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #19
dialogue.text: think.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #19
dialogue.text: There has to be a way.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #19
dialogue.text: The YANAGIBAKNIFE is probably too thin.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's not designed to cut through the tough skin and tendons needed during the filtering stage.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: My Deba knife is...but its blade is much too short.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #19
dialogue.text: Length. Sharpness. Sturdiness.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I need a blade with all three qualities for this to work!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #19
dialogue.text: What, still haven't done it?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and after all that bragging you did too.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #19
dialogue.text: Lame.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Excuse me? What're you even in here for anyway? That desperate for attention?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well, sorry, but I'm busy! Get outta my kitchen!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #20
dialogue.text: No need to tell me twice.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I've got my daily training to do anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #20
dialogue.text: But before I go...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...I need to leave one of my swords here.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #20
dialogue.text: This here is a big boy sword.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: "It won't listen to lame playboys who only know how to goof off.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: but I only know one guy like that. And I know if I left this sword in the same room as him...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...He wouldn't even have the guts to pick it up.

summary: This part advances the Wano / Rooftop Battle storyline through 41 dialogue beats, focusing on the raid’s momentum, character resolve, and the shifting battle lines.
